query: What Con Edison commercial customer service class pays the highest demand charge in Manhattan, New York City?
final_answer:
**Answer:** Assuming “demand charge” means the **maximum combined per‑kW demand-related charge on an active Con Edison commercial tariff in Manhattan/NYC Zone J**, the highest is:

### **SC‑9 – General Large Voluntary Time‑of‑Day Service – Zone J [NYC]**
- **Maximum demand-related rate:** **$76.38/kW-month**
- Calculated as:
  - **Flat/all-kW demand charge:** **$26.20/kW**
  - plus highest TOD demand component: **$40.17/kW + $10.01/kW adjustment = $50.18/kW**
  - **Total:** **$26.20 + $50.18 = $76.38/kW-month**
- Source: OpenEI Utility Rate Database API, active approved Consolidated Edison Co‑NY Inc. commercial tariffs for Manhattan address **350 5th Ave, New York, NY 10118**, tariff record **“SC-9 - General Large Voluntary TOD Service - Zone J [NYC]”**, utility EIA ID **4226**.

**Important caveat:** If you compare only a **single non-stacked demand-charge line item** rather than the total demand-related exposure under a TOD tariff, the highest line item in Manhattan/Zone J is **SC‑8 – Multiple Dwellings Redistribution Low Tension Service – Zone J [NYC]** at **$68.54/kW** (**$53.85/kW + $14.69/kW adjustment**), after the first 10 kW. Source: OpenEI Utility Rate Database API, same query.

I treated Manhattan as **NYC/Zone J**: NYISO’s load-zone map identifies **Zone J as N.Y.C.**, and Con Edison’s Market Supply Charge materials use zones **H, I, and J** with Zone J corresponding to NYC supply-zone selection. Sources: NYISO “New York Control Area Load Zones” map, https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1397960/nyca_zonemaps.pdf ; Con Edison Market Supply Charge Calculator page, https://www.coned.com/en/accounts-billing/your-bill/rate-calculators/market-supply-charge.

**Answer:** Assuming “demand charge” means the **maximum combined per‑kW demand-related charge on an active Con Edison commercial tariff in Manhattan/NYC Zone J**, the highest is:

### **SC‑9 – General Large Voluntary Time‑of‑Day Service – Zone J [NYC]**
- **Maximum demand-related rate:** **$76.38/kW-month**
- Calculated as:
  - **Flat/all-kW demand charge:** **$26.20/kW**
  - plus highest TOD demand component: **$40.17/kW + $10.01/kW adjustment = $50.18/kW**
  - **Total:** **$26.20 + $50.18 = $76.38/kW-month**
- Source: OpenEI Utility Rate Database API, active approved Consolidated Edison Co‑NY Inc. commercial tariffs for Manhattan address **350 5th Ave, New York, NY 10118**, tariff record **“SC-9 - General Large Voluntary TOD Service - Zone J [NYC]”**, utility EIA ID **4226**.

**Important caveat:** If you compare only a **single non-stacked demand-charge line item** rather than the total demand-related exposure under a TOD tariff, the highest line item in Manhattan/Zone J is **SC‑8 – Multiple Dwellings Redistribution Low Tension Service – Zone J [NYC]** at **$68.54/kW** (**$53.85/kW + $14.69/kW adjustment**), after the first 10 kW. Source: OpenEI Utility Rate Database API, same query.

I treated Manhattan as **NYC/Zone J**: NYISO’s load-zone map identifies **Zone J as N.Y.C.**, and Con Edison’s Market Supply Charge materials use zones **H, I, and J** with Zone J corresponding to NYC supply-zone selection. Sources: NYISO “New York Control Area Load Zones” map, https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1397960/nyca_zonemaps.pdf ; Con Edison Market Supply Charge Calculator page, https://www.coned.com/en/accounts-billing/your-bill/rate-calculators/market-supply-charge.
